description: PyZMQ is the official Python binding for ZeroMQ, a high-performance asynchronous messaging library.

documentation.example: import zmq; ctx = zmq.Context(); s = ctx.socket(zmq.PUB); s.bind('tcp://*:5555')
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pyzmq && python3 pyzmq.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pyzmq && python3 pyzmq.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pyzmq && python3 pyzmq.py --help`. Python bindings for the ZeroMQ messaging library.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the network category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python bindings for the ZeroMQ messaging library.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Python bindings for the ZeroMQ messaging library.

metadata.example: import zmq; ctx = zmq.Context(); s = ctx.socket(zmq.PUB); s.bind('tcp://*:5555')
